GMO / BE Status
(USDA NBFDS)

column4: From sugar cane; no synthetic adulterants
column6: Used within GMP; no maximum use level
column7:
Not BE listed (cane sugar)
No NBFDS disclosure required

column4: From sugar beet; highly refined — protein/DNA removed
column6: Used within GMP; no maximum use level
column7:
USDA NBFDS: Sugar beet IS a listed BE crop.
Highly refined sugar: BE disclosure may not be required if no detectable modified genetic material. Verify with USDA AMS guidance. IP CoA recommended.

column4: Enzymatically converted corn syrup; fructose fraction declared
column7: USDA NBFDS: Corn IS a listed BE crop. HFCS = highly refined; BE disclosure obligation depends on detectability. In practice most US HFCS is from BE corn — disclose unless Non-GMO IP CoA on file.

column4: Fat content tier declared; no adulteration
column6: Fat content tier must match application claim
column7: Cocoa is NOT on USDA BE list. No NBFDS disclosure required. Verify country-of-origin for EU export (EU GMO regulation Reg EC 1829/2003 may apply if cross-contamination risk).

column4: USP-grade corn starch for food use
column7: USDA NBFDS: Corn IS listed BE crop. Corn starch = refined. Disclosure required unless IP certified non-GMO. Require IP CoA + PCR test ≤0.9% if Non-GMO claim.

column4: Modification type listed (esterified, etherified, cross-linked)
column5: Modification type not in 21 CFR 172.892 list
column7: Same as Corn Starch — corn is BE listed. Disclosure required. IP option available.

column4: Protein ≥90% dry basis; allergen declared
column7: USDA NBFDS: Soybean IS listed BE crop. SPI retains detectable protein/DNA — BE disclosure MANDATORY unless Non-GMO Project Verified or USDA Organic. IP CoA + PCR test required for Non-GMO claim.

column4: Fully refined; from clean sound soybeans
column7: USDA NBFDS: Soybean IS listed BE crop. Highly refined soybean oil: detection of modified genetic material is unlikely after RBD process. USDA AMS guidance suggests highly refined oils may not require BE disclosure. Verify per current AMS guidance. IP CoA recommended.

column4: Fully refined, bleached, deodorized
column7: Palm is NOT on USDA BE list. No NBFDS disclosure required. Note: RSPO certification is a sustainability (not GMO) requirement.

column4: Erucic acid ≤2% total fatty acids
column5: Erucic acid >2% — FDA prohibits for human consumption
column7: USDA NBFDS: Canola/Rapeseed IS listed BE crop. Highly refined canola oil: similar to soybean oil — detectable modified material unlikely after refining. Verify per AMS guidance. For Non-GMO claim: IP CoA + PCR test required.

column4: Sodium chloride for food use within GMP
column5: Heavy metal contamination above Codex CXS 150
column7: Salt is NOT a biological material — NOT on USDA BE list. No GMO consideration.

column4: Ash ≤ (protein%/20)+0.35; enrichment per 137.165
column5: Enrichment not present; ash exceeds formula
column7: Wheat is NOT currently on USDA BE list (no commercially approved BE wheat in US market as of 2026). No NBFDS disclosure required. Monitor: HB4 drought-tolerant wheat may change status.

column4: Must derive from natural source only
column5: Synthetic aromatic chemical added; undeclared allergens
column6: Allergen sources in flavor must be declared
column7: May contain BE-derived carrier/solvent (e.g. corn-derived propylene glycol). Requires supplier declaration. NBFDS may apply if BE ingredient present above detectable threshold.

column4: Assay 98–102% dry; use within approved categories
column7: Sucralose is a synthesized sugar derivative — NOT a BE substance. No NBFDS disclosure required.

column1: Vitamin Premix (A, C, D, E, B)
column4: Each vitamin individually GRAS; potency declared
column5: Non-GRAS vitamin form; potency outside USP range
column7: Carriers/excipients in premix may be BE-derived (corn starch, soy). Require supplier GMO declaration for carrier materials. NBFDS applies to finished food if BE ingredient present.

column4: Protein ≥85% dry; from clean yellow peas
column7: Yellow peas are NOT on USDA BE list. No NBFDS disclosure required. Strong Non-GMO positioning — retain IP CoA and PCR test for marketing claims.

column4: Acetone insolubles ≥62%; emulsifier within GMP
column7: USDA NBFDS: Soybean IS listed BE crop. Lecithin retains protein/DNA material — BE disclosure REQUIRED unless Non-GMO Project Verified. IP CoA + PCR test required for Non-GMO claim.

column4: Approved low-GI carb; enzymatic conversion from sucrose
column6: Use in approved food categories only
column7: Derived from sucrose (cane or beet). If beet-derived: apply same sugar beet BE analysis. If cane-derived: not BE listed. Verify source with supplier.

column4: Low-acyl gellan gum; stabiliser at lowest effective level
column7: Gellan gum is produced via microbial fermentation (Sphingomonas elodea). Fermentation substrate may be BE-derived (corn glucose). Require supplier declaration on substrate origin. NBFDS application case-by-case per AMS guidance.

column4: L-Ascorbic Acid; assay 99.0–100.5% dry basis (USP); specific rotation −20.5° to −21.5°
column5: D-isoascorbic acid (erythorbic acid) substituted without declaration; assay outside USP range
column6: Used within GMP; no maximum use level for nutritive use; declared as '% DV Vitamin C' per 21 CFR 101.9
column7: Vitamin C (ascorbic acid) is a synthetic molecule — NOT a biological crop. NOT on USDA BE list. No NBFDS disclosure required. Note: Nature Made uses USP Verified Vitamin C — require USP Verification Program certificate or equivalent from supplier.

column4: Cholecalciferol (D3) from lanolin (ovine) or lichen (vegan); purity ≥98% (USP); declared potency in IU or mcg
column5: Ergocalciferol (D2) supplied when D3 specified; potency outside USP assay range (90–115% of label claim); irradiation not disclosed
column6: Declared as mcg or IU per serving; 1 mcg = 40 IU. Verify source: lanolin (bovine/ovine) or lichen (vegan claim). Must match label claim.
column7: Cholecalciferol is derived from animal (lanolin) or plant (lichen) sources — NOT a BE crop. NOT on USDA BE list. No NBFDS disclosure required. However, if carrier/diluent is corn starch or soy oil (common in D3 oil preparations): apply BE corn/soy rules to the carrier. Require full ingredient breakdown of D3 oil-based premix.

column4: d-alpha-Tocopherol (natural) ≥96.0% (USP); or d-alpha-Tocopheryl Acetate ≥96.0%. Must be natural form if labelled 'natural Vitamin E'.
column5: dl-alpha-Tocopheryl (synthetic) supplied when natural d-alpha specified — 1.36× potency difference; acid value >2.0 mg KOH/g; peroxide value >10 meq/kg
column6: Natural vs synthetic distinction is regulatory and commercial: label 'd-alpha' = natural (higher potency IU/mg); 'dl-alpha' = synthetic. Nature Made uses natural d-alpha. Specify form on PO.
column7: Tocopherol is typically derived from soybean oil distillate (most common), sunflower oil, or palm. USDA NBFDS: soy-derived tocopherol — soybean IS a BE-listed crop. Highly refined tocopherol: modified genetic material may not be detectable (similar to soybean oil). Verify per AMS highly refined guidance. For clean-label/Non-GMO: specify sunflower-derived tocopherol + IP CoA. This is Nature Made's direction — sunflower tocopherol is Non-GMO and allergen-free.

column4: Acetone insolubles ≥62% (FCC/AOCS); from clean sound sunflower seeds; no soy cross-contamination
column5: Acetone insolubles <62%; peroxide value >10 meq/kg; soy protein cross-contamination detected (allergen risk)
column6: Lecithin (any plant source) covered under 21 CFR 184.1400. No separate FDA approval needed for sunflower source. Must be labelled 'sunflower lecithin' (not just 'lecithin') if soy-free claim is made.
column7: ★ KEY ADVANTAGE OVER SOY LECITHIN: Sunflower is NOT on USDA BE list. No NBFDS disclosure required. No FALCPA soy allergen declaration needed. Clean label — growing consumer preference. Nature Made has shifted to sunflower lecithin in core SKUs. Require IP CoA confirming no cross-contamination with soy during processing (shared equipment). PCR screen for soy protein (ELISA) if soy-free claim is made.

column1: GMO COLUMN LEGEND  ·  ORANGE = BE disclosure obligation  ·  GREEN = no BE disclosure required  ·  YELLOW = verify with supplier / check AMS guidance  ·  Source: USDA NBFDS 7 CFR Part 66; AMS Highly Refined Oils Guidance (2022)
